ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-03-2011 Continued To May-10-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for and with Nancy Lawler. Paul Malone (Administrator) in Propria Persona.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to Mr. Malone's fee request and division of the statutory fees. Mr. Dimitriou stated that his objection was rejected by the filing Clerk because he did not have a notice of hearing, presented written objection to the fees to Mr. Malone and stated that he has additional fee requests. The matter was continued to May 10, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Dimitriou is to file papers regarding the bond issue by May 4, 2011. Mr. Malone is to file his response by May 6, 2011. Mr. Dimitriou's petition for extraordinary fees is to be filed by May 24, 2011.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-03-2011 Continued To May-10-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar May-03-2011 Continued To May-10-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-29-2011 Continued To May-03-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Malone (Administrator) in pro per. Hanesther Kim specially appearing for Andrew Dimitriou for and with Nancy Lawlor (Former Administrator).

The Court stated that it received Ms. Lawlor's declaration and that it understood that the accounting was filed yesterday. Mr. Malone stated that the assets were turned over, that the books were also to be turned over, that he needs the bank statements after 2007, the tax returns, last year's 1099's, the checkbook register, the inventories, and the bank statements. In addition, Mr. Malone stated that he knows that Ms. Lawlor has done her best. Ms. Lawlor stated that she will continue to search for the requested documents and that she knows the taxes were filed. Mr. Malone stated that he has filed a petition for final distribution covering the period the time that he took over and that he also filed a supplemental accounting. Mr. Malone stated that in looking at the accounting filed by Ms. Lawlor that he sees some discrepancies. Discussions regarding the monies from the lawsuit were held. The Court stated that the Examiner had pointed out some issues in January. Ms. Lawlor said that she addressed some of those issues in this accounting. Mr. Malone stated that the accounting that was filed won't do the trick. The Court stated that it was leaving the two petitions as set for May 3, 2011. Mr. Malone stated that they have been trying to get Mr. Dimitriou to amend the accounting and that he needs to resolve these issues. The Court requested that the documents be turned over to Mr. Malone as quickly as possible.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Mar-29-2011 Continued To May-03-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Feb-16-2011 Continued To Mar-29-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Malone for Heirs. Jonathan Ferdon for Public Administrator. Andrew Dimitriou for Nancy Lawler.

Mr. Dimitriou stated that the amended account had not been filed, that he should receive information from Schwab by next week and that the Estate is now in a position to be closed. Mr. Malone proposed that the letters issued to the previous Administrator be revoked , that letters be issued to him, requested that all information be turned over by Mr. Dimitriou and stated that he would file the petition for final distribution within 30-days. The Court removed Nancy Lawler as Administrator of the Estate. Mr. Dimitriou is to turn over all assets within 30-days and the amended account by Mr. Dimitriou is to be filed by March 29, 2011. The accounting and the Order to Show Cause were continued to March 29, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for Letters of Administration filed by Mr. Malone was granted (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-15-2010 Continued To Feb-16-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for and with Nancy Lawler. Paul Malone for Ulla Johansen.

Mr. Dimitriou and Ms. Lawler explained the difficulty in obtaining cooperation from Wells Fargo Bank with regard to producing statements, their efforts made in contacting and meeting with Wells Fargo, stated that the accounting was prepared with the information they had, that there was information missing and that the accounting needed to be reviewed by Ms. Lawler. Ms. Lawler also stated that she needed to contact other banks and obtain statements from the date of death to the time she opened the accounts. Mr. Dimitriou provided information regarding the Guadrow estate. The Court is combining the removal petition with the petition for appointment of Successor Administrator. All matters were continued to February 16, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Sanctions will continue to run and will be addressed on that date. All papers are to be filed by February 9, 2011.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Oct-13-2010 Continued To Dec-15-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for and with Nancy Lawlor. Paul Malone for Ulla Johansen.

Mr. Dimitriou requested a 60 day continuance stating that he was still missing some bank statements. Mr. Malone expressed concern about the numerous continuances, Mr. Dimitriou not complying with the Examiner's notes, stated that he wanted to see the accounting completed and verification of funds, and requested an increase in sanctions against Mr. Dimitriou. The order to show cause against Ms. Lawlor is dismissed. The matter was continued to December 15, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Malone's petition for removal and for letters is to be heard on that same date. Sanctions against Mr. Dimitriou continue to run at $25.00 per day. If that accounting is not filed, sanctions against Mr. Dimitriou will increase to $50.00 per day.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Sep-22-2010 Continued To Oct-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Malone for Ulla Yohansson.

Mr. Dimitriou was not present. The Court stated that Andrew Dimitriou has sanctions running against him, that the Bonding Company has advised the Court that the Administrator has failed to pay the bond premium and that sanctions continue to run. A further Order to Show Cause will be issued to the Administrator Nancy Lawlor and the Attorney Andrew Dimitriou to appear and explain why the First Account has not been filed, why the bond premium has not been paid, why sanctions against Mr. Dimitriou should not be increased to $50.00 per day and why sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day against Ms. Lawlor should not be imposed. The return date is October 13, 2010 at 9:00 a.m. , in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Aug-18-2010 Continued To Sep-22-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for Nancy Lawlor. Paul Malone for Lula Yohansen and most of the other Heirs at law.

Mr. Dimitriou stated that there was a calendar error in his office, that the delay was due to Ms. Lawlor not being available for personal reasons as well as his focus being directed to the $230,000.00 settlement payment, that he was trying to get a judgment against the Kudrow Estate, that he needed to figure out how to proceed and that he would file the accounting by the end of the week. Mr. Malone stated that all Parties were anxious to see the responses to the Examiner's notes. The matter was continued to September 22, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting is to be filed in proper format and served by September 7, 2010. If the accounting is not filed by said date, sanctions in the amount of $25.00 per day will begin to run on September 7, 2010.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar Jul-07-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Dimitriou for Nancy Lawlor. Paul Malone for Ulla Johansson.

Mr. Dimitriou withdrew his objection. The Court granted the petition (order for the same signed in open Court).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar Jun-23-2010 Continued To Jul-07-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Hanester Kim for Nancy Lawlor. Paul Malone for Ulla Johansson.

Hanester Kim requested a continuance stating that they had just received documents on Monday and that they believe their client deserves time to review and respond to them. Mr. Malone argued that normally he goes along with a reasonable continuance request but this case has been continued five times. In addition, he stated that there is an unsigned declaration in the file and that there have been copius Examiner's notes with explanations of how to correct the issues and how to proceed. Hanester Kim argued that the documents they received on Monday were evidentiary documents and that they need time to review them. The Court continued the matter to July 7, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 stating that this would be the last and final continuance. In addition, the Court stated that if Mr. Dimitriou wished to file a response that it is due by July 2, 2010.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar May-26-2010 Continued To Jul-21-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Malone for Ulla Johansson (Objector). Andrew Dimitriou for Nancy Lawlor.

Mr. Dimitriou requested that the matter be continued three weeks after the hearing on the Petition to Determine Heirship in order for him to know who he needs send notice to, stated that there was no way for him to bring the account current and provide notice timely. Mr. Malone had no problem with the accounting being heard after the Petition to Determine Heirship. Mr. Dimitriou stated that a hearing on the property issue was set for June 7, 2010 and that he would have a determination on that date, provided status of the property and stated that he would amend the accounting with regard to fees which are to be addressed when the Petition for Final Distribution is filed. Mr. Malone will provide copies of the documents (adoption, verification, genealogy) he receives from Sweden to Mr. Dimitriou and the Court. The accounting was continued to July 21, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Dimitriou is to address all issues and points raised in the Examiner's notes on that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Jan-06-2010 Continued To Mar-24-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Malone for Ulla Johansson. Andrew Dimitriou in Propria Persona.

Mr. Dimitriou stated that they had agreed to a three month continuance on the accounting, to a $20,000.00 payment/allowance for fees on account if acceptable to the Court and that Mr. Malone had filed a Petition for Determination of Heirship. The Court stated that the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to the accounting needed to be addressed, that it was not prepared to make an allowance on statutory compensation until the issues were cleared up, that it would entertain a stipulation regarding partial payment/allowance for the extraordinary fees and that an application for extraordinary fees should be at the same time of the petition for final distribution. Mr. Dimitriou also stated that time to comply with the settlement had expired, and that he would prepare a motion to comply with the settlement and to sell the property. The matter was continued to March 24, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-19-2008 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Conveying Real Property To Estate Of Stenornfelt Pursuant To California Probate Code 850 Et Seq
Off Calendar Apr-07-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jak Marquez for Sandra Gill (Respondent); Andrew Dimitriou for and with Nancy Lawlor (Petitioner).

Mr. Dimitriou reported that the settlement was not complied to and the client is deceased. His client is entitled to the house. Mr. Marquez stated that Ms. Gill is not available and her testimony was not taken, the abatement proceeding was filed, the property tax to be paid by Ms. Gill, and the personal property of Mr. Dimitriou's client remain in the house.
The Court denied the request for continuance by counsel. The 850 petition is abated until the conclusion of the Civil case #426996. The request to set aside dismissal order was never put back on the case management calendar. Party to file CCP664.6 motion.
Commissioner: Sue Kaplan; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: R. Hill; Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha/C. Herbert.
